mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through installing the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It provides seamless integration with Vultr's cloud features, such as automatic load balancer provisioning. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
The Vultr CLI is necessary for managing your Vultr resources from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --name my-cluster --region lon1 --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --plan vc2-4c-8gb

```

> Create a cluster in the London region with 3 nodes
commands: vultr-cli k8s create --name my-cluster --region lon1 --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --plan vc2-4c-8gb

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig my-cluster > ~/.kube/config

export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config

```

> Export the kubeconfig for the cluster
> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloud-provider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/master/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete my-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster and its resources

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer list

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete <loadbalancer-id>

```

> List any existing load balancers
> Delete any orphaned load balancers

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s backup my-cluster

vultr-cli k8s upgrade my-cluster --version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the cluster
> Upgrade the cluster to version 1.30
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or misconfigured kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure that the cluster is running with 'vultr-cli k8s list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the cluster to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or check resource requests/limits in your pod specifications.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the load balancer service is misconfigured or if there are quota limits on your account.

**Fix:**
Verify your load balancer service configuration and check your Vultr account quotas.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one or more nodes are not in a ready state, possibly due to initialization issues.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get nodes' to check the status and troubleshoot any nodes that are not ready.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr regions, improved load balancer provisioning.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: vultr-cli k8s create --name my-cluster --region lon1 --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --plan vc2-4c-8gb

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
